executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations compromised in a single day across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the campaign with 19 victims, followed by safepay (11), play (10), and clop (10). Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and telecommunications—this represents a national-scale cyber emergency requiring immediate coordinated response.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 19 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Pre-Con Builders, Bergmanis Preyra, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a catastrophic ransomware crisis with 103 organizations breached on June 17, 2026, across all provinces and sectors. Critical victims include public health agencies (Peterborough Public Health), municipal governments (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), education (Eastern Townships School Board, teccart.qc.ca, baisyaakov.ca), healthcare (Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, bisonfamilymedical.com), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period despite the unprecedented scale of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major consumer brand) breached by coinbasecartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross breached by sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database leaked by everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by sinobi; Trucash breached by lynx; Axion50plus breached by devman; multiple accounting and financial service firms compromised

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical Check Point VPN authentication bypass flaw (CVE-2026-50751) is being actively exploited in the wild, requiring immediate patching. Oracle PeopleSoft faces a suspected zero-day attack (CVE-2026-35273) with ShinyHunters threat actor involvement, though Oracle has not confirmed exploitation. The FBI disrupted a massive AI-powered phishing service using over one million URLs. Major breaches include the Council of Europe (297GB claimed by ShinyHunters), French government Tchap messenger (73,000+ employees), and pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk. A Chinese threat group exploited an Anthropic AI jailbreak to access Claude systems according to US government alerts.

items.description: Check Point VPN IKEv1 authentication bypass vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerability with possible zero-day exploitation reported by ShinyHunters
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Unknown, Availability: Unknown

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Council of Europe (297GB data claimed), Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273 exploitation)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: Akira Ransomware Group
items.targets: Paylogix (New York insurance tech company, breach occurred November 2025)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: Ink More (recent victim)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: SUB-ZER0
items.targets: French Ministry of Agriculture (3,400+ records claimed)
items.ttps: T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: Lapsus$
items.targets: GitHub (high-severity breach claimed, unconfirmed) via infostealer malware and credential compromise
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1555 (Credentials from Password Stores), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie)

items.name: Chinese APT (unnamed group)
items.targets: Anthropic Claude AI systems via jailbreak exploitation
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Brotherhood Capung Indonesia

items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: ShadowByt3$
items.targets: TinyPulse Nintendo (Nintendo.com)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System)

canada_landscape.summary: No CCCS (Canadian Centre for Cyber Security) official advisories were identified in this reporting period. Multiple references to Canadian political and cultural content were detected but no direct cybersecurity incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure, government systems, retailers, or financial institutions were reported. The threat environment affecting G7 allies (UK, France, US) suggests continued vigilance is warranted for similar attacks against Canadian targets.
